ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Alla kontrollpunkter stämmer överens mellan certifikatet och fakturan. Avsändare, mottagare, varubeskrivning, kvantitet och ursprungsland kan verifieras utan avvikelser. Dokumenten kan godkännas automatiskt.
overall_assessment.summary: Certificate of Origin SEG-26D-446869 verified against invoice 8500028201. All five checkpoints (consignor, consignee, goods description, quantity, country of origin) result in MATCH. The consignor SKF Eurotrade is identified as SKF Eurotrade AB in the invoice with matching address and country. The consignee EGYPTIAN ENGINEERING CO. matches exactly. The goods description HOUSING INCL BRG matches. Both Gross Weight (131,600 KG) and Net Weight (123,300 KG) match exactly. Country of origin Sweden is confirmed in the invoice's Country of manufacturing field and EU preferential origin declaration.
decision_factors: Consignor: SKF Eurotrade (cert) identified as SKF Eurotrade AB (invoice) - same entity, AB is legal form suffix per rule 4.1.3.6
Consignee: EGYPTIAN ENGINEERING CO. matches exactly in Bill to, Sold to, and Ship to fields
Goods: HOUSING INCL BRG matches in both documents - (SE) suffix in cert is non-identity-bearing country code
Quantity: GW 131,600 KG and NW 123,300 KG both match exactly between cert and invoice
Origin: Sweden in cert box 3 confirmed by Country of manufacturing: Sweden in invoice

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF. All fields legible. Original certificate with ICC verification code.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF, 4 pages. All fields legible. Issued by SKF Eurotrade AB.

document_pairing.method: Invoice number match and consignor match
document_pairing.notes: Certificate explicitly references invoice number 8500028201 and invoice date 22.01.2026. The invoice file contains invoice 8500028201 issued by SKF Eurotrade AB. Pairing is unambiguous.

criteria_met: Criterion 1: Certificate invoice reference 8500028201 matches invoice number 8500028201
Criterion 2: Certificate consignor SKF Eurotrade identified as SKF Eurotrade AB in invoice
Criterion 3: Certificate consignee EGYPTIAN ENGINEERING CO. identified in invoice Bill to / Sold to / Ship to

lc_check.reasoning: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate. The certificate contains references such as ACID, Lot Number, invoice number, and certificate number SEG-26D-446869. None of these constitute an LC reference. No field labeled 'L/C', 'Letter of Credit', 'Documentary Credit' or similar exists in the certificate.

locations: Invoice page 1: header 'SKF Eurotrade AB' and 'Issued by: SKF Eurotrade AB'
Invoice page 1: footer 'Issued by: SKF Eurotrade AB'
Invoice page 2: footer 'Issued by: SKF Eurotrade AB'
Invoice page 3: footer 'Issued by: SKF Eurotrade AB'
Invoice page 4: footer 'Issued by: SKF Eurotrade AB' and origin declaration 'Goteborg 22.01.2026 SKF Eurotrade AB'

comparison_steps.description: Search for certificate consignor name 'SKF Eurotrade' in all invoice pages
comparison_steps.result: FOUND - 'SKF Eurotrade AB' appears in invoice header, Issued by blocks, and footer on all 4 pages. The certificate name 'SKF Eurotrade' is contained within 'SKF Eurotrade AB'. The difference is only the legal form suffix 'AB'.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate: 'SKF Eurotrade' vs Invoice: 'SKF Eurotrade AB'. The only difference is the legal form suffix 'AB' (Aktiebolag). Per rule 4.1.3.6, differences consisting solely of legal form abbreviation are acceptable.
Certificate country: Sweden. Invoice country: Sweden (explicit in header address). Additionally confirmed by VAT prefix SE in SE556206761001 and IBAN prefix SE.

rules_applied.description: Koncernstruktur - consignor name found in invoice header/footer
rules_applied.result: Applicable - SKF Eurotrade found in invoice as SKF Eurotrade AB
Applied - difference is only legal form suffix AB

rules_applied.description: Indirect land verification via VAT prefix SE

checkpoints.motivation: The certificate states consignor as 'SKF Eurotrade' with address '415 50, GÖTEBORG, Sweden'. The invoice is issued by 'SKF Eurotrade AB' with address '415 50 Goteborg, Sweden'. The company name differs only by the legal form suffix 'AB' (Aktiebolag), which is an accepted variation per rule 4.1.3.6. The core name 'SKF Eurotrade' is identical. The country Sweden matches exactly. The VAT number SE556206761001 appears in both documents, further confirming identity. GÖTEBORG vs Goteborg is a minor variation (Swedish special characters). Result: MATCH.
checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate text: 'SKF Eurotrade / 415 50, GÖTEBORG / Sweden'. Invoice text: 'SKF Eurotrade AB / 415 50 Goteborg / Sweden'. Step 2b search confirmed 'SKF Eurotrade' found on all 4 invoice pages. Rule 4.1.0 satisfied. Rule 4.1.3.6 applies for the 'AB' suffix. Rule 4.1.4 satisfied with exact country match. Additional confirmation via matching VAT number SE556206761001 in both documents (cert box 5, invoice footer).

checkpoints.certificate_value: EGYPTIAN ENGINEERING CO., B4 Piece 23-Industrial City, 7064518 10th of Ramadan city, Egypt
checkpoints.invoice_value: EGYPTIAN ENGINEERING CO., B4 Piece 23-Industrial City, 7064518 10th of Ramadan city, Egypt

comparison_steps.description: Invoice contains primary billing fields: 'Bill to' and 'Sold to' both showing EGYPTIAN ENGINEERING CO. This is the authoritative consignee per priority 1.
Certificate: 'EGYPTIAN ENGINEERING CO.' vs Invoice Bill to: 'EGYPTIAN ENGINEERING CO.' - exact match after normalization.
Certificate: Egypt. Invoice Bill to: Egypt. Exact match.

rules_applied.description: Priority identification - Bill to field used as primary
rules_applied.result: Bill to field contains EGYPTIAN ENGINEERING CO.

checkpoints.motivation: The certificate states consignee as 'EGYPTIAN ENGINEERING CO.' with address 'B4 Piece 23-Industrial City, 7064518 10th of Ramadan city, Egypt'. The invoice's Bill to, Sold to, and Ship to fields all show 'EGYPTIAN ENGINEERING CO.' with the identical address 'B4 Piece 23-Industrial City, 7064518 10th of Ramadan city, Egypt'. Company name, address, and country match exactly. Result: MATCH.
checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate text: 'EGYPTIAN ENGINEERING CO. / B4 Piece 23-Industrial City / 7064518 10th of Ramadan city / Egypt'. Invoice Bill to text: 'EGYPTIAN ENGINEERING CO. / B4 Piece 23-Industrial City / 7064518 10th of Ramadan city / Egypt'. Exact character-for-character match. All three invoice address blocks (Bill to, Sold to, Ship to) contain the same party.

comparison_steps.description: No article numbers are stated in the certificate. Proceeding to Priority 2.
comparison_steps.result: Not applicable - no article numbers in certificate

comparison_steps.description: Certificate goods description: 'HOUSING INCL BRG(SE)'. The identity-bearing main designation is 'HOUSING INCL BRG'. The '(SE)' suffix is a country code per rule 4.3.4 (non-identity-bearing addition - country designation). Invoice goods description: 'HOUSING INCL BRG' (page 1 product table, page 2 customs declaration, page 3 line items). Exact match of the identity-bearing main designation.

The '(SE)' suffix in the certificate is a country code designation. Per rule 4.3.4, country designations (SE, EU) are non-identity-bearing additions that shall not cause MISMATCH when the identity-bearing main designation matches.

rules_applied.result: (SE) is a country designation, ignored per rule

checkpoints.motivation: The certificate describes the goods as 'HOUSING INCL BRG(SE)'. The identity-bearing main designation is 'HOUSING INCL BRG'. The suffix '(SE)' is a country code indicating origin Sweden, which is a non-identity-bearing addition per rule 4.3.4. The invoice describes the goods as 'HOUSING INCL BRG' on pages 1, 2, and 3. The identity-bearing main designation matches exactly. The HS code 84832000 in the invoice is additional information consistent with bearings/housing products. Result: MATCH.
checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate text: 'HOUSING INCL BRG(SE)'. Invoice text: 'HOUSING INCL BRG' (appears on invoice page 1 in Country of manufacturing table, page 2 in Customs declaration table, page 3 in line item details all three lines). After removing non-identity-bearing (SE) suffix, the texts are identical. Rule 4.3.4 explicitly allows country designations to be ignored.

checkpoints.certificate_value: Gross Weight: 131,600 KG / Net Weight: 123,300 KG
checkpoints.invoice_value: Total Gross Weight 131,600 KG / Total Net Weight 123,300 KG

comparison_steps.description: Certificate box 7 contains two weight values: Gross Weight 131,600 KG and Net Weight 123,300 KG. These are the verifying quantities. The certificate also mentions '1 package of:' in box 6 (shipment quantity per 4.4.3.2).
comparison_steps.result: Box 7 values identified as primary verification quantities

comparison_steps.description: Certificate values 131,600 KG and 123,300 KG use comma as thousands separator (both are integers with exactly 3 digits after comma). Invoice page 3 states 'Total Gross Weight 131,600 KG' and 'Total Net Weight 123,300 KG' with same notation. These are whole numbers where comma serves as thousands separator.
comparison_steps.result: Normalization applied - comma as thousands separator

comparison_steps.description: Certificate: 131,600 KG (GW). Invoice page 3: Total Gross Weight 131,600 KG. After normalization: 131600 KG = 131600 KG. Exact match.
Certificate: 123,300 KG (NW). Invoice page 3: Total Net Weight 123,300 KG. Additionally invoice page 1 total row: Net kg 123,300. After normalization: 123300 KG = 123300 KG. Exact match.
Both GW and NW in certificate match the corresponding values in invoice. No contradiction exists.

rules_applied.result: GW and NW values from box 7 used
Comma normalized as thousands separator for integer values
Both GW and NW match exactly
Invoice Total Gross Weight and Total Net Weight match certificate

checkpoints.motivation: The certificate states Gross Weight: 131,600 KG and Net Weight: 123,300 KG in box 7. The invoice states Total Gross Weight 131,600 KG and Total Net Weight 123,300 KG on page 3 (Transport details). The comma in '131,600' and '123,300' serves as a thousands separator (both values are integers: 131600 and 123300). After normalization per rule 4.4.3.3, both GW and NW match exactly. The invoice page 1 total row also confirms Net kg 123,300. No contradiction exists between any values. Both weight categories (GW and NW) are verified. Result: MATCH.
checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate GW: '131,600 KG' → normalized 131600 KG. Invoice Total GW: '131,600 KG' → normalized 131600 KG. Exact match. Certificate NW: '123,300 KG' → normalized 123300 KG. Invoice Total NW: '123,300 KG' → normalized 123300 KG. Exact match. Invoice page 1 also shows Net kg total 123,300 confirming NW. Both weight categories verified per 4.4.2.2 and 4.4.4.

checkpoints.invoice_value: Country of manufacturing: Sweden; EU preferential origin declaration

comparison_steps.description: Invoice page 1 contains column header 'Country of manufacturing' with value 'Sweden'. This is an explicit origin field. Additionally, invoice page 4 contains EU preferential origin declaration: 'these products are of EU preferential origin'. Invoice page 3 line items show country code 'SE' (Sweden) for all three product lines.
Certificate: '.Sweden' (with leading dot, likely formatting artifact). Normalized to 'Sweden'. Invoice: 'Sweden'. Exact match after normalization.

Certificate origin 'Sweden' is explicitly identified in invoice's 'Country of manufacturing' field as 'Sweden'. Additionally confirmed by country code 'SE' on all line items and EU preferential origin declaration (Sweden is EU member). No contradicting origin information found.

rules_applied.result: Country of manufacturing: Sweden identified on page 1
Sweden is EU member - consistent with EU preferential origin declaration
Sweden explicitly found in dedicated origin field

checkpoints.motivation: The certificate states country of origin as '.Sweden' (box 3 — the leading dot is a formatting artifact). The invoice contains an explicit 'Country of manufacturing' column on page 1 with value 'Sweden'. All three line items on page 3 show country code 'SE' (Sweden) in the 'Country of manufacturing' column. Additionally, invoice page 4 contains an EU preferential origin declaration, which is consistent with Sweden being an EU member state. The origin 'Sweden' is explicitly and unambiguously identified in the invoice. No contradicting origin information exists. Result: MATCH.
checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3: '.Sweden'. After normalization (removing leading dot): 'Sweden'. Invoice page 1 column header 'Country of manufacturing' → value 'Sweden'. Invoice page 3 line items all show 'SE' in Country of manufacturing column. Invoice page 4: 'these products are of EU preferential origin' — Sweden is EU member (post-Brexit EU-27 list includes SE). All three sources confirm Swedish origin consistently.

evidence.raw_text: The exporter of the products covered by this document (customs authorization No. SE/GBG/002172) declares that, except where otherwise clearly indicated, these products are of EU preferential origin.

rule_evaluation_log.details: Document A contains ICC Certificate of Origin with certificate number SEG-26D-446869. Document B contains commercial invoice 8500028201 (4 pages). Certificate references invoice 8500028201 which matches the invoice file.

Criterion 1: cert invoice ref 8500028201 = invoice number 8500028201. Criterion 2: cert consignor SKF Eurotrade found as SKF Eurotrade AB in invoice. Criterion 3: cert consignee EGYPTIAN ENGINEERING CO. found in invoice Bill to.

rule_evaluation_log.details: Certificate consignor 'SKF Eurotrade' found as substring of 'SKF Eurotrade AB' on all 4 invoice pages in header and footer.

rule_evaluation_log.result: Applied - AB suffix is legal form
rule_evaluation_log.details: SKF Eurotrade (cert) vs SKF Eurotrade AB (invoice). Difference is only 'AB' (Aktiebolag). Core name identical.
Certificate: Sweden. Invoice: Sweden (header address) + SE prefix in VAT + SE prefix in IBAN.

rule_evaluation_log.result: Bill to field identified as primary
rule_evaluation_log.details: Invoice contains explicit 'Bill to' field with EGYPTIAN ENGINEERING CO. This is priority 1.
EGYPTIAN ENGINEERING CO. matches exactly in cert and invoice.
Certificate: Egypt. Invoice Bill to: Egypt.
Certificate: HOUSING INCL BRG(SE). Main designation: HOUSING INCL BRG. Invoice: HOUSING INCL BRG. Exact match.
(SE) after HOUSING INCL BRG is a country designation. SE corresponds to Sweden which matches cert origin. Ignored per rule.

rule_evaluation_log.result: Applied - box 7 contains GW and NW
rule_evaluation_log.details: Box 7 contains Gross Weight 131,600 KG and Net Weight 123,300 KG. Box 6 contains '1 package of:' (shipment quantity).
131,600 and 123,300 are integers with comma as thousands separator (exactly 3 digits after comma, no decimal part). Normalized to 131600 and 123300.
Certificate GW 131,600 KG = Invoice Total Gross Weight 131,600 KG.
Certificate NW 123,300 KG = Invoice Total Net Weight 123,300 KG.

rule_evaluation_log.result: No contradiction - both GW and NW match
rule_evaluation_log.details: GW cert=131600 KG, invoice=131600 KG. NW cert=123300 KG, invoice=123300 KG. Both match.
Invoice page 1 has explicit 'Country of manufacturing' column with value 'Sweden'. All line items show 'SE'.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The comma in 131,600 and 123,300 could be a decimal separator
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: In the context of this invoice, the comma functions as a thousands separator. Evidence: (1) Invoice page 1 total row shows 'Net kg 123,300' alongside quantity '20' — 123.3 kg for 20 housing units would be unrealistically light (~6.2 kg each), while 123,300 kg (123.3 metric tons) is also unrealistic for 20 bearings. However, examining the invoice monetary values shows comma used consistently as thousands separator (e.g., '3.602,60' EUR uses period as thousands and comma as decimal). The weight values '131,600' and '123,300' follow the pattern of period-less integers where comma is thousands separator (exactly 3 trailing digits). Both documents use the same notation, confirming consistency. The fact that both cert and invoice show identical strings '131,600 KG' and '123,300 KG' means the values are identical regardless of interpretation.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The consignor name mismatch (SKF Eurotrade vs SKF Eurotrade AB) could indicate different entities
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: The difference is exclusively the legal form suffix 'AB' (Aktiebolag, Swedish limited company). Per rule 4.1.3.6, this is an accepted variation. The core name 'SKF Eurotrade' is identical. Both documents share the same address (415 50, Göteborg/Goteborg, Sweden) and the same VAT number (SE556206761001). There is no indication of different legal entities.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The goods description mismatch (HOUSING INCL BRG(SE) vs HOUSING INCL BRG) could indicate different products
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: The '(SE)' suffix is a country code (Sweden) appended to the goods description in the certificate to indicate origin per article per rule 4.3.7. Per rule 4.3.4, country designations are non-identity-bearing additions. The identity-bearing main designation 'HOUSING INCL BRG' is identical in both documents.

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot Faktura

sections.rule_applied: Skillnaden avser enbart juridisk bolagsform (AB = Aktiebolag). Kärnnamnet 'SKF Eurotrade' är identiskt. Samma VAT-nummer SE556206761001 bekräftar att det är samma juridiska part.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets consignor 'SKF Eurotrade' identifieras i fakturan som 'SKF Eurotrade AB'. Enda skillnaden är bolagsformssuffixet 'AB'. Land: Sverige i båda dokumenten. Bekräftas ytterligare av matchande VAT-nummer.

sections.certificate_value: EGYPTIAN ENGINEERING CO., B4 Piece 23-Industrial City, 7064518 10th of Ramadan city, Egypt
sections.invoice_value: EGYPTIAN ENGINEERING CO., B4 Piece 23-Industrial City, 7064518 10th of Ramadan city, Egypt
sections.rule_applied: Exakt överensstämmelse av företagsnamn, adress och land.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets mottagare 'EGYPTIAN ENGINEERING CO.' med adress i Egypten matchar exakt fakturans Bill to-, Sold to- och Ship to-fält.

sections.rule_applied: Identitetsbärande huvudbeteckning 'HOUSING INCL BRG' matchar exakt. Suffixet '(SE)' är en landskodsbeteckning som inte bär produktidentitet.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets varubeskrivning 'HOUSING INCL BRG(SE)' identifieras i fakturan som 'HOUSING INCL BRG'. Landskoden (SE) efter huvudbeteckningen är ett icke-identitetsbärande tillägg.

sections.certificate_value: Gross Weight: 131,600 KG / Net Weight: 123,300 KG
sections.invoice_value: Total Gross Weight 131,600 KG / Total Net Weight 123,300 KG
sections.rule_applied: Båda viktkategorierna (brutto och netto) överensstämmer exakt efter normalisering av tusentalsavskiljare.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets bruttovikt 131,600 KG och nettovikt 123,300 KG matchar exakt fakturans Total Gross Weight 131,600 KG och Total Net Weight 123,300 KG (sida 3, transportuppgifter).

sections.rule_applied: Ursprungslandet 'Sweden' identifieras explicit i fakturans 'Country of manufacturing'-fält. Stöds ytterligare av EU-ursprungsdeklaration.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets ursprungsland '.Sweden' (formaterad med inledande punkt) matchar fakturans 'Country of manufacturing: Sweden'. Alla artikelrader visar landskod 'SE'. EU-ursprungsdeklaration på sida 4 är konsistent med svenskt ursprung.

output_quality_checks.confidence_floor_details: All applicable checkpoint confidences >= 0.85. Minimum: 0.96 (goods description).
output_quality_checks.consistency_note: All 5 checkpoints result in MATCH. comparison_result = IDENTICAL is consistent. workflow_recommendation = AUTO_APPROVAL_ELIGIBLE is consistent with IDENTICAL result and no blockers.
